title: Cross-build linux releases w/ arch in filename
body:
Depends on #11631

Fixes #11417

Cross-building the podman-remote documentation requires a functional
native architecture executable.  However `make` only deals with
files/timestamps, it doesn't understand if an existing binary will
function on the system or not.  This makes building cross-platform
releases incredibly accident-prone and fragile.

A practical way to deal with this, is via multiple conditional (nested)
`make` calls along with careful manipulation of `$GOOS` and `$GOARCH`.
Also, when cross-building releases be kind to humans and cleanup
any non-native binaries left behind.

Update the `Alt Arch. Cross` Cirrus-CI task to build release archives
for all Linux architectures supported by golang and podman.  Update
the `OSX Cross` task to additionally build for the M1 (arm64)
architecture.

Finally, update the release process documentation to reflect the
new locations (Cirrus-CI task names) for the release archives.  Include
a note about additional manual work being required to produce the
signed `.dmg` file for MacOS.

comments.body:
***Review notes:*** My eyes are now fully crossed, and I need help looking this over with fresh vision-orbs.

* I'm not happy about using `if` conditions and calling `$(MAKE)` inside targets, but couldn't find an easy way to deal with `bin/podman` != `bin/podman` when it comes to multi-arch :disappointed: It seems like unless the logic is there, it's *way* too easy to silently screw things up.
* We could maybe use a naming convention to simplify things a little, i.e. output `bin/<arch>/podman` (one idea).  But I fear this will complexify the overall Makefile vs. just using an `if` just in the release-related targets.
* I'm kind of wishy/washy on cleaning up non-native binaries at the end of the release targets.  It doesn't really affect automation one way or another, but could impact humans working specifically on feature requiring lots of release cross-builds (regular cross-compiles aren't affected).
